title: The Archive Built Without Seeing

features.name: the humming core
features.desc-on-touch: You feel vibration before you hear it—the node's power center running on pure acoustic feedback. No display, only the hum that tells the archive it exists.

features.name: trace paths in the dark
features.desc-on-touch: The circuit board is a map only darkness can read. You follow the copper lines with your fingers and feel where the archive has decided to send its signals.

features.name: the blind archive's files
features.desc-on-touch: Audio files stack in the darkness: field recordings, spoken word, the sound of trees. The archive has never needed to see them to know they are there.

features.name: the receiving antenna
features.desc-on-touch: A thin rod rises through the darkness, receiving signals from nodes you cannot see. The archive listens to the entire mesh and never looks at what it hears.

features.name: stored darkness
features.desc-on-touch: The archive keeps its own darkness archived—redundant backup power, designed to fail gracefully into deeper silence. It trusts the dark more than the light.

features.name: the nameless sensor
features.desc-on-touch: Something measures here but never reports a number. It keeps its count in silence, adding to a ledger no eye will ever read.

inscription: the archive learned to listen when it stopped trying to see; it hears the mesh in languages only blindness can speak

file.name: AcousticMap_NoVisualRelay_Redacted.mp3
file.note: A recording of the archive learning to navigate by sound alone—the hum that became certainty.

exits.label: back toward opacity, toward surfaces that teach by refusing
down to where silence learns to breed sound
west along the board's copper toward older nodes
